meta.summary: Add backward-compatible preset orchestration profiles, resolve one concrete vv-controller policy from the startup config snapshot, preserve prompt-only execution semantics, and expose profile management through the CLI and diagnostics.
meta.affected_modules: M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES, M-CLI-CONFIG, M-VVOC-PRESET-REGISTRY, M-CLI-PRESET, M-PLUGIN-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION, M-PLUGIN-WORKFLOW, M-CLI-MANAGED-AGENTS, M-CLI-MANAGED-SKILLS, M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION, M-CLI-COMMANDS, M-CLI-COMPLETION
meta.complexity: High: the change is backward-compatible but crosses canonical config/schema, preset mutation, runtime prompt injection, managed prompts and skills, CLI/status/completions, public documentation, and durable GRACE graph and verification projections.

module.name: OrchestrationProfiles
module.purpose: Own profile names, default resolution, validation, profile capabilities, and the exact concrete controller policies consumed by runtime plugins.
module.contract: Remain independent of VvocConfig to avoid dependency cycles. Accept structurally compatible config input, resolve missing orchestration to balanced, reject explicit invalid profile values, and expose only the selected policy to callers.

module.name: CanonicalConfigAndPresets
module.purpose: Extend strict schema v3 with optional root and preset orchestration fields, seed balanced defaults, preserve old valid v3 documents, and atomically apply preset roles plus declared profile.
module.contract: Keep schema version 3 and the current required-section list. A missing root section resolves to balanced. Built-in presets always declare a profile. User presets may omit it, in which case applyPreset preserves the current root profile. Validate the entire selected preset before a single write.

module.name: ProfileAwareRuntimeGuidance
module.purpose: Inject universal primary guidance plus one concrete vv-controller orchestration policy and one compatible workflow protocol without changing tool permissions or tracked workflow enforcement.
module.contract: Only vv-controller receives orchestration policy. Other eligible primaries receive universal guidance only. Single-session receives direct-work plus reviewer-only mechanics, balanced receives selective delegation guidance, and orchestrated receives the full tracked workflow. All existing work-item tools and task hooks remain behaviorally unchanged.

module.name: ManagedPromptSurface
module.purpose: Remove inactive orchestration branches from the always-loaded controller prompt while preserving universal safety, spec/plan gates, skill routing, and explicit vv-review and vv-execute semantics.
module.contract: The controller template must not enumerate profile-specific agent routes or tracked loops. The vv-execute frontmatter description must not expose inline/classic alternatives before the skill is loaded, while the skill body must retain its explicit user choice. vv-review remains unchanged and findings-only.

module.name: OrchestrationCLI
module.purpose: Provide scoped show/set operations, register the command, add shell completions, and make effective profile and source visible in status.
module.contract: show follows global/project/effective read semantics and reports source. set accepts only global/project writes, validates one of the three profile values, preserves unrelated config, and prints restart guidance. status reports the resolved profile alongside the selected vvoc source without mutating invalid config.

module.name: PublicDocumentation
module.purpose: Document profile semantics, built-in preset mappings, CLI usage, prompt-only limitations, restart requirements, reviewer exception, and vv-execute behavior.
module.contract: README remains the source of truth for public CLI, setup, config, and workflow behavior.

module.name: GraceArchitectureState
module.purpose: Keep module ownership, dependencies, data flows, critical flows, and verification commands truthful after adding orchestration profiles and the new CLI command.
module.contract: Add deterministic M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES and M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION ownership plus matching V-M anchors, update existing config/preset/system-context/workflow/commands/completion dependencies, and record profile resolution and prompt isolation as a critical flow.

wave.goal: Establish the orchestration profile contract and backward-compatible config and preset behavior.

task.title: Orchestration profile domain model
task.description: Create the internal profile module and its colocated test file. Define stable profile names, balanced fallback, strict parsing, resolved capabilities, and exact concrete controller policy strings. Keep the module independent of VvocConfig and update MODULE_CONTRACT, MODULE_MAP, CHANGE_SUMMARY, and focused block/function contracts.

acceptance.criterion: ORCHESTRATION_PROFILE_NAMES contains exactly single-session, balanced, and orchestrated in that order
resolveOrchestrationPolicy returns balanced when orchestration is absent
parseOrchestrationProfile rejects blank and unknown values with all allowed choices in the error
Each resolved controllerSystemContext contains only its active profile instructions and does not enumerate the other profile names
single-session instructs direct exploration, investigation, planning, implementation, and verification while permitting independent reviewer subagents under the approved conditions
balanced assigns selective workflow guidance and orchestrated assigns tracked workflow guidance without inspecting model data

task.title: Backward-compatible v3 config schema
task.description: Integrate orchestration into VvocConfig and VvocPreset, seed explicit balanced root defaults, preserve optional raw-v3 compatibility during parse, render normalized sections, and extend both runtime and published schemas. Strengthen opencode and config-validate tests for old documents, round trips, exact JSON paths, and managed preset refresh.

acceptance.criterion: The runtime and published v3 schemas accept root orchestration.profile and preset orchestration.profile with only the three supported values
orchestration remains absent from the schema required list so an existing valid v3 document without it still validates
Parsing a valid old v3 document without orchestration produces effective balanced behavior
Fresh config rendering includes root orchestration.profile balanced and every built-in preset includes its declared profile
Unknown root and preset profiles fail with /orchestration/profile or /presets/<name>/orchestration/profile in validation output
syncVvocConfig preserves the selected root profile, refreshes managed preset profiles, and preserves custom preset orchestration
The schema $id and config version remain unchanged except for the normal package-versioned schema URL behavior

task.title: Preset operating-profile application
task.description: Extend the built-in registry and preset command so managed presets declare the approved mapping, selected preset roles and profile are validated before mutation, and one write applies all declared changes. Preserve the current root profile for model-only custom presets. Update preset formatting, list/apply output, restart guidance, and tests.

acceptance.criterion: Each built-in preset exposes exactly the approved orchestration mapping
Applying a built-in preset updates roles and root profile in one file write and never rewrites OpenCode config
Applying a custom preset without orchestration reports unchanged and preserves the current root profile
Applying a preset whose roles are kept but profile changes still writes the new profile
Any invalid role or profile leaves the original vvoc.json byte content unchanged
CLI output reports each role action, orchestration updated/kept/unchanged state, target path, and restart guidance when config changed

wave.goal: Make the runtime and managed prompt surface profile-aware without harness-level enforcement.

task.title: Concrete controller system-context injection
task.description: Separate universal primary guidance from vv-controller orchestration policy. Remove proactive explore and all-profile route language from universal contexts, resolve the startup policy once, append only the active controller context to vv-controller, preserve explore-specific worker guidance and subagent exclusion, and update positive/negative tests for all profiles.

acceptance.criterion: vv-controller receives universal guidance plus exactly one controllerSystemContext selected from the startup snapshot
single-session output does not contain proactive explore, investigator, vv-implementer, change_with_review, or tracked implementation-loop instructions
single-session output contains the short explicit no-working-subagent rule and the reviewer exception
build and custom primary agents receive universal guidance without any orchestration policy
explore continues receiving only its compact search-worker guidance and known subagents receive no primary context
Repeated hook invocation does not duplicate universal or orchestration contexts
Changing vvoc.json after plugin construction does not change the selected policy

task.title: Profile-compatible workflow guidance
task.description: Resolve the same startup profile in WorkflowPlugin and select reviewer-only, selective, or full tracked guidance for vv-controller. Keep system-instruction.md as the orchestrated protocol source, add concise single-session and balanced guidance without inactive loops, and leave tool registration, access control, task interception, state, persistence, repair, and transition behavior unchanged.

acceptance.criterion: single-session guidance explains only tracked review-only mechanics, one-round default, findings validation, and no implementer use for findings
balanced guidance permits bounded explore, investigator, mechanical implementer, and reviewer use without making any route mandatory or creating automatic loops
orchestrated guidance preserves the existing full work-item implementation and review protocol
review-only requests remain findings-only and do not route reviewer FAIL results to vv-implementer
All existing tracked launch, result parsing, repair, persistence, hard-stop, and round-limit tests continue to pass unchanged in behavior
Workflow tools and guidance remain restricted to vv-controller

task.title: Profile-neutral managed controller prompt
task.description: Rewrite the always-loaded controller template around universal invariants only. Preserve findings-first behavior, language matching, assumptions, fresh verification, rerouting, direct editing rules, skill triggers, approved spec/plan gates, hard-stop handoffs, and final reporting. Remove route tables and packet/loop instructions that belong only to balanced or orchestrated policies. Update managed agent prompt assertions.
task.snippet:
<core_principles> - Own the user-facing task end to end and present analysis, plans, investigations, and reviews before implementation. - Match the user's language, preserve repository terminology, state material assumptions, and require fresh verification. - Follow the concrete system work policy supplied for this session; do not invent alternative orchestration rules. </core_principles>
<skill_trigger_rule> - vv-spec creates and approves a specification. - vv-plan creates an implementation plan and does not implement. - vv-review performs findings-only independent review and does not fix without user confirmation. - vv-execute follows its own explicit user-selected execution mode. </skill_trigger_rule>

acceptance.criterion: The controller template contains universal core, working-state, assumption, reroute, editing, skill, large-feature gate, hard-stop, plan artifact, and final-response guidance
The template does not prescribe explore, investigator, vv-implementer, required reviewer loops, implementation work items, or change_with_review as ordinary routes
The template explicitly defers concrete working behavior to the selected system work policy without describing inactive profiles
vv-review remains findings-only and vv-execute remains an explicit specialized workflow
Managed prompt sync and template loading tests continue to recognize vv-controller as one primary agent

task.title: Managed skill visibility regression coverage
task.description: Create focused tests for vv-review and vv-execute and make the vv-execute frontmatter description neutral before skill loading. Preserve the full inline/classic choice and workflows inside the skill body. Do not change vv-review behavior.

acceptance.criterion: The vv-execute YAML description does not contain inline, classic, subagent-driven, or current-session alternatives
The loaded vv-execute body still defines inline and classic, requires explicit user choice, and performs no mutation before choice
The loaded vv-review body still opens review_only work items, dispatches only reviewers, treats FAIL as findings, and stops before fixes
Managed skill template loading remains project/global compatible

wave.goal: Expose profile management and effective diagnostics through the public CLI.

task.title: Scoped orchestration command
task.description: Create show/set commands and tests using existing config read/write scope conventions. show resolves effective fallback and source without mutation. set strict-validates an existing document or bootstraps a fresh canonical config, updates only root orchestration logically, preserves unrelated sections and custom presets, rejects effective writes, and prints restart guidance.

acceptance.criterion: show defaults to effective scope and prints profile plus env/project/global/default source
show resolves a missing root section as balanced without writing the file
set accepts exactly the three profile values and supports only global or project writes
set returns kept without rewriting when the explicit profile already matches
set updates only orchestration logically while preserving roles, guardian, secretsRedaction, presets, and plugins
set rejects an invalid existing vvoc.json without changing its byte content
set bootstraps a canonical v3 config when the selected path is absent
Successful CLI set output includes the target path and restart OpenCode guidance

task.title: CLI registration and shell completion
task.description: Register the orchestration command and extend bash, zsh, and fish completions with show/set and the three profile values. Update completion tests and command metadata/contracts without changing unrelated commands.

acceptance.criterion: vvoc orchestration is registered as a top-level command
Bash, zsh, and fish suggest show and set after orchestration
Bash, zsh, and fish suggest all three profile values after orchestration set
Top-level command completion expectations remain synchronized with src/cli.ts

task.title: Effective profile status reporting
task.description: Extend InstallationInspection with resolved orchestration information and print it in status beside the selected vvoc source. Preserve diagnostic-only behavior for invalid configs and show balanced for the effective default source. Update status and inspection tests.

acceptance.criterion: Valid global and project status reports print the resolved orchestration profile
Effective status with no vvoc file reports balanced and source default
Status reports the profile from the selected source rather than another layer
Invalid explicit profile is reported as a parse problem and the selected file remains unchanged
Existing role, Guardian, secrets-redaction, source, warning, and problem output remains intact

wave.goal: Synchronize public documentation and durable GRACE architecture and verification state, then run release-grade gates.

task.title: Public orchestration documentation
task.description: Update feature, plugin, CLI, model-role/preset, config, managed-agent, and managed-skill documentation. Explain concrete profile semantics, mapping table, partial preset behavior, prompt-only enforcement, reviewer exception, vv-execute explicit choice, effective source display, and restart requirement.
task.snippet:
## Orchestration Profiles
- `single-session`: vv-controller performs exploration, investigation, planning, implementation, and verification directly; independent reviewer subagents remain available under the documented review conditions. - `balanced`: vv-controller owns critical reasoning and may selectively delegate bounded search, investigation, mechanical implementation, or review. - `orchestrated`: vv-controller uses the full tracked implementer/reviewer workflow.
```bash vvoc orchestration show --scope effective vvoc orchestration set single-session --scope project ```

acceptance.criterion: README documents every new command and profile value exactly as implemented
README includes the approved built-in preset mapping and states that user presets without orchestration preserve the current profile
README states that profiles are prompt-driven in the first version and do not disable task or workflow tools
README distinguishes reviewer subagents from delegated working context and preserves vv-review findings-only semantics
README states that vv-execute still asks for inline/classic and that config changes require an OpenCode restart

task.title: GRACE module and data-flow projection
task.description: Update graph/index.xml and graph/main.xml with direct anchors, module entries, dependencies, paths, and data flow. Add M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES and M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION; update existing config, preset, system-context, workflow, commands, managed prompts/skills, and completion descriptions and dependencies.
task.snippet:
<M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES><Name>OrchestrationProfiles</Name><Type>CORE_LOGIC</Type><Status>implemented</Status><Purpose>Resolve preset-configured orchestration profiles into one concrete vv-controller policy and workflow capability set.</Purpose><Paths>src/lib/orchestration.ts</Paths><Depends>none</Depends><Verification>V-M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES</Verification></M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES>
<M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION><Name>OrchestrationCommand</Name><Type>ENTRY_POINT</Type><Status>implemented</Status><Purpose>Show and set scoped orchestration profiles with source and restart diagnostics.</Purpose><Paths>src/commands/orchestration.ts</Paths><Depends>M-CONFIG-LAYERS, M-CLI-CONFIG, M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES</Depends><Verification>V-M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION</Verification></M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION>
<DF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE>Preset or CLI writes a profile, startup config resolves it once, and prompt plugins inject only the concrete vv-controller and workflow guidance for that profile.</DF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE>

acceptance.criterion: Both new M anchors appear in graph/index.xml ownership, routed-document direct anchors, ModuleAnchors, and Modules
Every changed source and template file is owned by an accurate graph module
Existing module dependencies include M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES where runtime/config behavior now depends on it
DF-PRESET-SWITCH, DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT, DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE, and the new DF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE describe current behavior truthfully

task.title: GRACE verification projection and final gates
task.description: Update verification/index.xml and verification/main.xml with matching direct anchors, module verification entries, critical flow expectations, test files, and release-grade commands. Run all targeted suites followed by GRACE lint/status and repository checks.
task.snippet:
<VF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE><Flow>DF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE, DF-PRESET-SWITCH, DF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT, DF-WORKFLOW-GUIDANCE</Flow><Priority>high</Priority><Expected>Effective startup config resolves one profile; vv-controller receives only its concrete policy; old v3 config defaults to balanced; preset writes are atomic; invalid explicit values fail without fallback.</Expected></VF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE>
<V-M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES><TestFiles><File>src/lib/orchestration.test.ts</File></TestFiles><Checks><Command>bun test src/lib/orchestration.test.ts</Command><Command>bun run typecheck</Command></Checks></V-M-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILES>
<V-M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION><TestFiles><File>src/commands/orchestration.test.ts</File></TestFiles><Checks><Command>bun test src/commands/orchestration.test.ts</Command><Command>bun run build</Command></Checks></V-M-CLI-ORCHESTRATION>

acceptance.criterion: New V anchors appear in verification/index.xml ownership, routed-document direct anchors, VerificationAnchors, and ModuleVerification
VF-ORCHESTRATION-PROFILE verifies backward compatibility, atomic writes, source resolution, prompt isolation, and fail-closed explicit values
Existing preset, system-context, workflow, config, commands, managed agents/skills, completion, and release verification entries include the new test ownership and expectations
All targeted tests for orchestration, config validation, presets, system context, workflow, managed prompts/skills, CLI, completion, status, and config inspection pass
bunx @osovv/grace-cli@rc lint --path . and bunx @osovv/grace-cli@rc status --path . pass
bun run release:check, bun run check, bun run build, and bun run pack:check pass with fresh output

verification.command: bun test src/lib/orchestration.test.ts src/lib/opencode.test.ts src/lib/managed-agents.test.ts src/lib/managed-skills.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/orchestration.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts src/commands/status.test.ts src/plugins/system-context-injection.test.ts src/plugins/workflow.test.ts && bunx @osovv/grace-cli@rc lint --path . && bunx @osovv/grace-cli@rc status --path . && bun run release:check && bun run check && bun run build && bun run pack:check
